comment: Jurisdiction knowledge base. status is one of: verified | draft | not_researched. Only Fred flips an entry to verified, after review with Urbatec. Citations containing <<verify>> cause the engine to downgrade that rule's determinations to 'depends' at render time. The non-schema 'research' block records provenance for quarterly re-verification; see research/REVIEW.md for the full review sheet.

product_default.name: Product default (jurisdiction not researched)
product_default.comment: Generic product logic used when a jurisdiction is unknown or not_researched. Makes no jurisdiction-specific claims and never outputs likely_exempt (bias to the safe error).

rules.rationale: Structures at or under the common 120 sqft line are often exempt from a building permit, but this varies by city — we'll confirm yours. The dedicated 50A circuit typically needs an electrical permit.
Most jurisdictions require a building permit above the common 120 sqft accessory-structure exemption line.
Any water/sewer connection tends to reclassify the pod toward a dwelling unit (DADU), which triggers full residential code.
Sleeping use pushes toward habitable-space / dwelling-unit classification in some jurisdictions.

jurisdictions.name: Seattle

construction_requirements.requirement: Conditioned residential buildings in Seattle (Climate Zone Marine 4) must meet 2021 Seattle Energy Code Table R402.1.3 prescriptive minimums: ceiling R-60, wood-frame wall R-20 cavity + R-5 continuous (or R-13 + R-10 ci), floor R-30, slab-on-grade R-10 to 4 ft depth, and vertical fenestration (window) U-0.30 max. Same values as the 2021 WSEC-R state code.

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Energy Code, Residential Provisions, Table R402.1.3
citation.title: Seattle Energy Code Residential, Chapter 4 (2021)
citation.snippet: TABLE R402.1.3 INSULATION MINIMUM R-VALUES AND FENESTRATION REQUIREMENTS BY COMPONENTS — CLIMATE ZONE 5 AND MARINE 4: Fenestration U-Factor 0.30; Ceiling R-Value 60; Wood Frame Wall R-Value 20+5 or 13+10; Floor R-Value 30; Slab R-Value & Depth 10, 4 ft

construction_requirements.requirement: As an alternative to R-values, assemblies may comply by maximum U-factor per 2021 Seattle Energy Code Table R402.1.2 (Climate Zone 5 and Marine 4): ceiling U-0.024, above-grade wall U-0.056, floor U-0.029, fenestration U-0.30, slab-on-grade F-0.54.

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Energy Code, Residential Provisions, Table R402.1.2
citation.title: Seattle Energy Code Residential, Chapter 4 (2021)
citation.snippet: TABLE R402.1.2 INSULATION AND FENESTRATION REQUIREMENTS BY COMPONENT — CLIMATE ZONE 5 AND MARINE 4: Fenestration U-Factor 0.30; Ceiling U-Factor 0.024; Above-Grade Wall U-Factor 0.056; Floor U-Factor 0.029; Slab on Grade F-Factor 0.54

construction_requirements.requirement: A heated/cooled (conditioned) accessory building must comply with the full building thermal envelope provisions of the 2021 Seattle Energy Code; only buildings with no conditioned space, or with a peak space-conditioning design load under 3.4 Btu/h per sq ft (1.0 W/sq ft), are exempt from envelope requirements (SEC R402.1 exceptions).

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Energy Code, Residential Provisions, Section R402.1
citation.title: Seattle Energy Code Residential, Chapter 4 (2021)
citation.snippet: The following buildings, or portions thereof ... shall be exempt from the building thermal envelope provisions of this code: 1. Those with a peak design rate of energy usage less than 3.4 Btu/h • ft2 ... 2. Those that do not contain conditioned space.

construction_requirements.requirement: Energy code compliance documentation is required for heated residential projects in Seattle: duct-leakage and heating-system sizing forms must be submitted with the permit (plus the Total UA Alternative form if that compliance path is used), and an Insulation Certificate must be posted in the completed building. SDCI directs applicants to the 2021 WSEC-R worksheets on the WSU Energy Program site.

citation.code_section: SDCI Energy Code compliance forms (2021 Seattle Energy Code / 2021 WSEC-R)
citation.title: Energy Code - Forms - SDCI | seattle.gov
citation.snippet: You must submit duct-leakage forms and heating-system sizing forms. If you use the Total UA Alternative path, you must submit that form as well.

construction_requirements.requirement: Roofs in Seattle must be designed for a uniform snow load of at least 25 psf (Seattle amendment, 2021 Seattle Building Code 1608.1). The Seattle Residential Code Table R301.2 lists a ground snow load (pg) of 20 psf, with a uniform flat roof snow load of 25 psf permitted as the alternate design value.

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Building Code Section 1608.1 (Seattle amendment); see also 2021 Seattle Residential Code Table R301.2
citation.title: 2021 Seattle Building Code, Chapter 16, Structural Design
citation.snippet: [S] 1608.1 General. Roofs shall be designed for a uniform snow load of at least 25 psf (1200 Pa). Design snow loads shall be determined in accordance with Chapter 7 of ASCE 7

construction_requirements.requirement: Basic (ultimate, 3-second gust) design wind speed for Seattle residential construction is 98 mph per 2021 Seattle Residential Code Table R301.2, with 'Yes' for topographic wind speed-up effects and 'No' for special wind region and wind-borne debris zone.

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Residential Code, Table R301.2 (Climatic and Geographic Design Criteria), Wind Design - Speed
citation.title: 2021 Seattle Residential Code, Chapter 3, Building Planning
citation.snippet: TABLE R301.2 CLIMATIC AND GEOGRAPHIC DESIGN CRITERIA ... 20 psf ... 98 ... Yes ... No ... No ... D2 ... Moderate ... 12"

construction_requirements.requirement: Seattle residential construction is Seismic Design Category D2 per 2021 Seattle Residential Code Table R301.2 — the most restrictive category under the IRC prescriptive provisions, which triggers seismic bracing/anchorage detailing (e.g., stem wall reinforcement per R403.1.3 applies in SDC D0-D2).

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Residential Code, Table R301.2, Seismic Design Category
citation.title: 2021 Seattle Residential Code, Chapter 3, Building Planning
citation.snippet: SEISMIC DESIGN CATEGORY ... D2 ... Moderate ... 12"

construction_requirements.requirement: Concrete footings must be at least 12 in wide x 6 in thick, and exterior footings must bear at least 12 in below undisturbed ground surface on undisturbed natural soil or engineered fill (2021 Seattle Residential Code R403.1.1 and R403.1.4). Seattle's frost line depth in Table R301.2 is 12 inches.

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Residential Code Sections R403.1.1 and R403.1.4
citation.title: 2021 Seattle Residential Code, Chapter 4, Foundations
citation.snippet: R403.1.4 Minimum depth. Exterior footings shall be placed not less than 12 inches (305 mm) below the undisturbed ground surface.

construction_requirements.requirement: Frost protection of foundations is NOT required for free-standing light-frame accessory structures of 600 sq ft or less with eave height 10 ft or less (2021 Seattle Residential Code R403.1.4.1 Exception 1) — this covers typical 96-300 sq ft office pods, so footings need not extend below the 12-in frost line, though minimum footing depth/bearing rules still apply.

citation.code_section: 2021 Seattle Residential Code Section R403.1.4.1, Exception 1
citation.title: 2021 Seattle Residential Code, Chapter 4, Foundations
citation.snippet: Protection of free-standing accessory structures with an area of 600 square feet (56 m2) or less, of light-frame construction, with an eave height of 10 feet (3048 mm) or less shall not be required.

construction_requirements.requirement: Seattle exempts from building permit only one-story detached accessory buildings with projected roof area under 120 sq ft on a slab-on-ground foundation — smaller than the 200 sq ft IRC default — and the exemption does not apply in environmentally critical areas (ECAs); exempt or not, the structure must still meet all code requirements and development standards.

citation.code_section: SDCI permit exemption policy (SRC R105.2 as amended by Seattle)
citation.title: Do You Need a Permit? - SDCI | seattle.gov
citation.snippet: A one-story detached accessory building such as a greenhouse, tool or storage shed, playhouse, or similar building if the projected roof area is less than 120 square feet and the building foundation is only a slab on the ground

jurisdictions.adopted_codes: 2021 Seattle Building Code (2021 IBC + Seattle amendments); 2021 Seattle Residential Code (2021 IRC + amendments); 2021 Seattle Energy Code (largely matches 2021 WSEC); 2021 WA State Plumbing Code w/ Seattle amendments (enforced by Public Health — Seattle & King County). Seattle Electrical Code edition <<verify>>.

citation.code_section: SDCI guidance — 'Sheds' common projects page (underlying SBC/SRC exemption section pending verification)
citation.title: Sheds — SDCI
citation.snippet: Only used for storage, growing plants, or similar generally unoccupied uses

rules.rationale: SDCI's shed exemption (single story, roof footprint 120 sqft or less, simple foundation, not in an ECA) is limited to generally unoccupied uses like storage. A conditioned, occupied office pod may not qualify even at 96 sqft — confirm with SDCI before skipping a permit. SDCI requires an electrical permit any time wiring is installed, altered, extended, or connected, so the 50A feeder needs one regardless. Zoning standards still apply even without a permit.

citation.code_section: SDCI guidance — 'Sheds' common projects page (underlying SBC/SRC exemption section pending verification)
citation.title: Sheds — SDCI
citation.snippet: the shed's roof is 120 square feet or less

rules.rationale: Above 120 sqft the exemption is unavailable; structures typically need at least a subject-to-field-inspection construction permit (full permit if in/near an ECA, over 750 sqft, or beams spanning over 14 ft). Applies to the Sixteen and the Station. Zoning is reviewed as part of the permit.

citation.code_section: Seattle Plumbing Code (2021 WA State Plumbing Code with Seattle amendments)
citation.title: Plumbing and gas piping applications and permits — Public Health, Seattle & King County
citation.snippet: install, relocate, or change a plumbing or gas piping system

rules.rationale: Plumbing disqualifies the shed exemption, so a building permit applies at any footprint. Plumbing permits are issued by Public Health — Seattle & King County (206-263-9566), not SDCI. With sleeping plus kitchen/bath the pod crosses into DADU territory (see sleeping rule).

citation.code_section: SMC 23.42.022 (referenced by SDCI ADU page; full code text not yet archived)
citation.title: Accessory Dwelling Unit — SDCI
citation.snippet: a legally permitted unit on the property (but not within the home)

rules.rationale: A pod used for sleeping is a separate living space under Seattle's DADU framework: full construction permit plus current residential, building, electrical, energy, and land use codes. Tiny houses on foundations are explicitly classified as DADUs. Pre-approved DADU plans (ADUniverse) can shorten approval.

citation.code_section: <<verify>>
citation.title: Accessory Dwelling Unit — SDCI
citation.snippet: Tiny houses on wheels are treated like camper trailers.

rules.rationale: Seattle treats tiny houses on wheels like camper trailers and prohibits living in them on city lots — so a trailer-mounted pod used for sleeping is effectively prohibited as a dwelling. Treatment of a trailer-mounted, non-sleeping office pod was not found on any official page; a hard-wired 50A feeder still implicates SDCI's electrical permit rule. Confirm with SDCI before relying on vehicle treatment.

jurisdictions.zoning_note: Per the SDCI sheds page (NR1–NR3 zones): exempt sheds limited to 12 ft tall; keep structures out of the front setback (typically the first 10–15 ft) and at least 5 ft from side property lines; combined structure coverage roughly 50–60% of lot (verify exact formula in SMC 23.44 <<verify>> — Municode blocked archiving). DADUs governed by SMC 23.42.022 and zone height/location standards.
jurisdictions.hoa_note: An HOA may impose separate requirements regardless of city rules.
jurisdictions.notes: DRAFT (researched 2026-06-09, official SDCI/King County pages). Key escalators: (1) the 120 sqft exemption requires 'generally unoccupied uses' — a conditioned office may not qualify at any size, confirm with SDCI; SDCI pages phrase the line as both 'less than 120' and '120 or less' — exact boundary <<verify>>. (2) Seattle runs its OWN electrical permitting through SDCI (not WA L&I) — every pod's 50A inlet triggers it. (3) Plumbing permits go through Public Health — Seattle & King County, not SDCI. (4) Sleeping = DADU = full permit. (5) ECA proximity voids the exemption. Validation anchor: live permit (Anna Chen). Electrical inspections: (206) 684-8950.

research.method: Official sources fetched and read during a supervised research session; see research/REVIEW.md and research/sources/wa-king-seattle/.
caveats: SMC Ch. 23.44 dimensional tables unfetchable (Municode 403) — setback/coverage details from SDCI summary pages only.
SDCI Tip 312 returned 404 and is NOT cited.
Seattle Electrical Code adopted edition not verified.

jurisdictions.name: San Jose

construction_requirements.requirement: San Jose is in California Energy Commission (CEC) Building Climate Zone 4 for Title 24 Part 6 compliance; San Jose-Reid is the representative city/weather station for Climate Zone 4.

citation.code_section: CEC Building Climate Zones (Title 24 Part 6, Section 100.1 climate zone designations)
citation.title: Building Climate Zones - California State Geoportal (California Energy Commission)
citation.snippet: The representative city for each climate zone (CZ) is: ... CZ 4: San Jose-Reid

construction_requirements.requirement: The 2025 California Energy Code (Title 24 Part 6) applies to building permit applications submitted on or after January 1, 2026; projects must use 2025 compliance software and 2025 energy compliance documents (CF1R for single-family residential scope, NRCC for nonresidential scope).

citation.code_section: 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards (Title 24, Part 6)
citation.title: 2025 Energy Code Overview (California Energy Commission)
citation.snippet: Effective January 1, 2026 — Building permit applications submitted on or after effective date — Must use 2025 software and compliance documents

construction_requirements.requirement: Under the 2025 Energy Code single-family residential standards (Section 150.0(c)), mandatory minimum wall insulation is U-factor 0.095 or R-15 for 2x4 walls and U-factor 0.069 or R-21 for 2x6 walls (all climate zones, including CZ4/San Jose).

citation.code_section: 2025 CA Energy Code Section 150.0(c)
citation.title: 2025 Energy Code Overview (California Energy Commission)
citation.snippet: 150.0(c) — Updates mandatory wall insulation: 2x4 walls U-factor 0.095 or R-15; 2x6 walls U-factor of 0.069 or R-21

construction_requirements.requirement: Under the 2025 Energy Code single-family residential standards, windows have a mandatory maximum U-factor of 0.40 in all climate zones (Section 150.0(q)) and a prescriptive maximum U-factor of 0.27 in climate zones 1-5 (which includes San Jose, CZ4) per Section 150.1(c)3.

citation.code_section: 2025 CA Energy Code Sections 150.0(q) and 150.1(c)3
citation.title: 2025 Energy Code Overview (California Energy Commission)
citation.snippet: 150.0(q) — Updates mandatory U-factor 0.40 in all climate zones; 150.1(c)3 — Updates prescriptive U-factor 0.27 in climate zones 1-5, 11-14, 16

construction_requirements.requirement: San Jose Building Division inspection guidelines (2022 CRC edition): footings for light-frame residential construction must conform to CRC Table R403.1(1) minimums — not less than 12 inches wide, 6 inches thick, with minimum 2-inch projection.

citation.code_section: CRC Table R403.1(1) as applied by City of San Jose
citation.title: Construction Guidelines for Residential Construction (City of San Jose Building Division)
citation.snippet: Footing depth & dimensions conform to plan and minimum code requirements (Table R403.1(1)), not less than 12" in width (W), 6" in depth (T) and min. 2" projection (P)

construction_requirements.requirement: San Jose's local frost line depth is 5 inches per the Building Division's local climatic and geographic design criteria — frost protection does not govern footing depth (the CRC 12-inch minimum below undisturbed ground controls).

citation.code_section: CRC Table R301.2(1) local design criteria (City of San Jose)
citation.title: Construction Guidelines for Residential Construction (City of San Jose Building Division)
citation.snippet: Frost Line Depth: 5 inches

construction_requirements.requirement: Design ground snow load for San Jose is 0 psf per the City of San Jose Building Division's local climatic and geographic design criteria (2022 CRC/CBC edition).

citation.code_section: CRC Table R301.2(1) local design criteria (City of San Jose)
citation.title: Construction Guidelines for Residential Construction (City of San Jose Building Division)
citation.snippet: Ground Snow Load 0

construction_requirements.requirement: Basic (ultimate) design wind speed for San Jose is 95 mph per the City of San Jose Building Division's local design criteria (2022 CRC/CBC edition; Risk Category II ultimate design wind speed).

citation.code_section: CRC Table R301.2(1) local design criteria (City of San Jose)
citation.title: Construction Guidelines for Residential Construction (City of San Jose Building Division)
citation.snippet: Wind Design Speed (mph) 95 Ultimate

construction_requirements.requirement: Seismic Design Category for residential construction in San Jose is D1 through E per the City of San Jose Building Division's local design criteria (2022 CRC edition) — high-seismic detailing and braced-wall/engineered-design provisions apply.

citation.code_section: CRC Table R301.2(1) local design criteria (City of San Jose)
citation.title: Construction Guidelines for Residential Construction (City of San Jose Building Division)
citation.snippet: Seismic Design Category D1 through E

construction_requirements.requirement: San Jose Bulletin #250 (12/09/2019): ordinary accessory buildings on single-family/duplex lots are limited to one story, limited to two plumbing fixtures, may not contain living or sleeping spaces, and cannot be air conditioned (SJMC similarly limits them to unconditioned space as defined in Title 24). A heated/cooled office pod therefore does not qualify as an ordinary accessory building under this bulletin and needs a different classification or permit path.

citation.code_section: San Jose Bulletin #250; SJMC Sec. 20.200.020 / 20.30.500
citation.title: Bulletin #250 - Accessory Buildings and Structures (City of San Jose)
citation.snippet: Limited to one story ... Limited to two plumbing fixtures ... May not contain any living or sleeping spaces ... Cannot be air conditioned

construction_requirements.requirement: San Jose accessory buildings are limited to 12 feet maximum height (if pitched roof: 12 feet measured halfway up the slope, 16 feet maximum at the roof peak); total coverage by all accessory buildings/structures including detached garage is capped at 650 sq ft unless a Special Use Permit is granted (Bulletin #250, 12/09/2019).

citation.code_section: San Jose Bulletin #250 - Size and dimension requirements
citation.title: Bulletin #250 - Accessory Buildings and Structures (City of San Jose)
citation.snippet: Maximum height: 12 feet. If the roof is pitched, the height halfway up any slope may be 12 feet and the roof peak may be 16 feet maximum.

construction_requirements.requirement: If a San Jose accessory building exceeds 120 sq ft and is less than 5 feet from the property line, walls parallel to the property line must have 1-hour fire protection; within 3 feet of the property line no wall openings are allowed, between 3-5 feet openings are limited to 25%, and overhangs may not come closer than 2 feet to the property line (Bulletin #250, 12/09/2019).

citation.code_section: San Jose Bulletin #250 - 1-hour fire protection walls / Openings and overhangs
citation.title: Bulletin #250 - Accessory Buildings and Structures (City of San Jose)
citation.snippet: Exceeds 120 sf and is less than 5 feet from the property line, walls parallel to the property line must be built with a 1-hour fire protection.

authority.dept: Building Division, Dept. of Planning, Building and Code Enforcement (Permit Center, 200 E. Santa Clara St.)

jurisdictions.adopted_codes: 2025 California Building Standards Code (CCR Title 24) effective Jan 1, 2026, incl. 2025 CA Residential Code (Part 2.5), Electrical (Part 3), Plumbing (Part 5), Energy (Part 6); adopted with local amendments in SJMC Title 24.

citation.code_section: SJMC 24.02.110 (as summarized in City Bulletin #201, 06/12/2025)
citation.title: Bulletin #201 — When Is a Building Permit Required?
citation.snippet: 120 square feet or less and that will not be electrically wired

rules.rationale: San Jose's 120 sqft exemption applies only if the structure will NOT be electrically wired. Every Mini·O pod ships with the standardized 50A inlet, so the exemption cannot apply at any size — Bulletin #250 states that with electrical or plumbing improvements 'a permit is required regardless of the size.' Zoning is 'depends' because Bulletin #250 says accessory buildings 'Cannot be air conditioned' — a conditioned pod may need planner review (free consults: ZoningQuestions@sanjoseca.gov).

citation.code_section: SJMC 24.02.110; SJMC 20.30.500 (zoning)
citation.title: Bulletin #250 — Accessory Buildings and Structures (04/26/2024)
citation.snippet: If the planned size is greater than 120 square feet, a building permit is required.

rules.rationale: A building permit is directly required above 120 sqft. The 160 and 300 sqft pods fit under the 650 sqft Special Use Permit trigger, but the accessory-building limits (one story, no sleeping space, no air conditioning) may push a conditioned pod toward ADU classification or planner review.

citation.code_section: SJMC 24.02.110 (permit exemptions); SJMC 20.30.500 (accessory building standards)
citation.title: Bulletin #250 — Accessory Buildings and Structures (04/26/2024)
citation.snippet: a permit is required regardless of the size of the structure

rules.rationale: Any plumbing improvement requires permits regardless of size. Accessory buildings are 'Limited to two plumbing fixtures' — a full bath (3 fixtures) or kitchenette-plus-bath exceeds that, pointing toward ADU classification under Bulletin #210 (SJMC 20.80.175/176).

citation.code_section: SJMC 20.30.500 (accessory buildings); SJMC 20.80.175/20.80.176 (ADU standards per Bulletin #210)
citation.title: Bulletin #250 — Accessory Buildings and Structures (04/26/2024)
citation.snippet: May not contain any living or sleeping spaces

rules.rationale: Ordinary accessory buildings may not contain sleeping spaces, so a sleeping pod must go through the ADU process. Caution: San Jose's ADU minimum is 150 sqft — the 96 sqft Twelve is below it and also disqualified as a sleeping accessory building; there may be no lawful sleeping configuration for the Twelve. The Sixteen and Station clear the minimum.

citation.code_section: City Bulletin #210 (ADU Universal Checklist, 10/10/2025); references Bulletin #291 (THOW)
citation.title: Bulletin #210 — ADU Universal Checklist
citation.snippet: This is a prefabricated ADU mounted on a trailer chassis

rules.rationale: San Jose regulates tiny homes on wheels as ADUs with a dedicated permit checklist (Bulletin #291) — not as unregulated vehicles. That regime covers dwelling units; treatment of a non-habitable trailer-mounted office pod is unverified. Confirm with the Permit Center before relying on trailer mounting to avoid permits.

jurisdictions.zoning_note: Non-ADU accessory buildings (Bulletin #250, SJMC 20.30.500/510/530): one story; max 12 ft height (16 ft roof peak); 60 ft front setback; no side/rear setback on interior lots (4 ft key lots, 10 ft street-side corners); 6 ft from other buildings; max 650 sqft total without Special Use Permit; rear-yard coverage max 40% all structures / 30% accessory buildings; 1-hr fire-rated walls if over 120 sqft within 5 ft of property line; max two plumbing fixtures; NO sleeping spaces; 'Cannot be air conditioned' — major flag for conditioned pods. ADUs (Bulletin #210): min 150 sqft; detached max 1,000–1,200 sqft (city) / 800 sqft (state); 4 ft side/rear setbacks (state standards); max 18 ft one story; no parking required.
jurisdictions.hoa_note: An HOA may impose separate requirements regardless of city rules.
jurisdictions.notes: DRAFT (researched 2026-06-09). VERIFICATION CAVEAT: live sanjoseca.gov pages block automated fetching; all cited content was read from Internet Archive captures of the same official URLs (captures 2025-03 to 2026-03) — re-confirm against live pages during verification. Key escalators: (1) the exemption requires NO electrical wiring — every pod needs permits at any size; (2) accessory buildings may not be air conditioned — conditioned pods likely need planner consultation even without plumbing; (3) the Twelve (96 sqft) is below the 150 sqft ADU minimum — no clear lawful sleeping configuration; (4) THOWs are regulated as ADUs. Bulletins predate the Jan 2026 switch to 2025 codes — re-confirm thresholds (408-535-3555). Bulletin #250 is ambiguous at exactly 120 sqft ('greater than 120' vs '120 or greater') — treat exactly-120 as permit-required. Validation anchor: live permit (Gary Deng), record 2026-121660-RS, plumbing A/B (one pod no plumbing, one 3/4 bath).

research.method: Official city bulletins and pages read via Internet Archive captures of official sanjoseca.gov URLs (live site blocks automation); see research/REVIEW.md.
caveats: All citations verified via Internet Archive captures, not live pages (WAF blocks bots).
SJMC code text itself unfetchable (Municode 403) — sections cited as referenced by city bulletins.
Bulletin #291 (Tiny Home on Wheels checklist) referenced but not locatable.
Original research posture for the under-120 rule was building_permit: depends; upgraded to likely_required during assembly because the no-wiring condition is directly cited and every pod is wired.

jurisdictions.name: Del Mar

construction_requirements.requirement: Del Mar has adopted the 2025 California Energy Code (Title 24, Part 6) via Ordinance 1022, effective 10-22-2025; all construction of buildings where energy will be utilized must conform, so a conditioned backyard office pod must comply with the 2025 Energy Code.

citation.code_section: Del Mar Municipal Code 23.12.050
citation.title: Chapter 23.12 - California Codes for Construction, Division 5 - Energy Code
citation.snippet: There is adopted and incorporated by reference herein as the city energy code for the purpose of prescribing regulations in the City of Del Mar for the conservation of energy the 2025 California Energy Code, Part 6, Title 24 of the California Code of Regulations

construction_requirements.requirement: 2025 Energy Code prescriptive envelope package for single-family residential in Climate Zone 7 (Table 150.1-A): ceiling insulation R-30 for vented attics (Option C) with radiant barrier required; above-grade framed wall assembly U-factor max 0.065; raised floor R-19 (U-0.037); no slab-edge insulation requirement; fenestration max U-0.30 and max SHGC 0.23; opaque doors max U-0.20; Quality Insulation Installation (QII) required.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code, Table 150.1-A and Section 150.1(c)
citation.title: 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards for Residential and Nonresidential Buildings (CEC-400-2025-010-F), pp. 429-430
citation.snippet: Above Grade Framed Walls3 U 0.048 U 0.048 U 0.048 U 0.048 U 0.048 U 0.065 U 0.065

construction_requirements.requirement: 2025 Energy Code mandatory (floor) insulation minimums for newly constructed single-family residential buildings, regardless of compliance path: ceilings/rafter roofs U-0.043 max or R-22 between framing; 2x4 walls U-0.095 max (R-15 demonstrates compliance) and 2x6 walls U-0.069 max (R-21 demonstrates compliance); raised floors U-0.037 max or R-19.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code, Sections 150.0(a), 150.0(c), 150.0(d)
citation.title: 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards (CEC-400-2025-010-F), Section 150.0 Mandatory Features and Devices
citation.snippet: Ceilings and rafter roofs shall be insulated to achieve an area-weighted average U-factor not exceeding U-0.043 or shall be insulated between wood-framing members with insulation resulting in an installed thermal resistance of R-22 or greater for the insulation alone.

construction_requirements.requirement: A Title 24 Part 6 compliance report is required: the building permit application must include energy compliance documentation (Certificate of Compliance, CF1R, per Part 1 Sections 10-103(a)1 and 10-103(a)2), demonstrating the design meets either the prescriptive package (Table 150.1-A) or the performance energy budget for Climate Zone 7 under the 2025 Energy Code.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code, Section 150.1(b)2A
citation.title: 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards (CEC-400-2025-010-F), Section 150.1
citation.snippet: The application for a building permit shall include documentation pursuant to Sections 10-103(a)1 and 10-103(a)2 which demonstrates, using an approved calculation method, that the building has been designed so that its energy consumption does not exceed the standard design energy budgets for the applicable climate zone.

construction_requirements.requirement: Under the 2025 Energy Code prescriptive path, space heating must be a heat pump (electric resistance heating is not allowed as the primary heating system in any climate zone, including CZ7); a non-heat-pump system requires the performance compliance path. A supplemental heater up to 2 kW / 7,000 Btu/hr on a 30-minute time-limiting control is permitted.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code, Section 150.1(c)6 and Table 150.1-A
citation.title: 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards (CEC-400-2025-010-F), Section 150.1
citation.snippet: Heating system type. Heating system types shall be installed as required in Table 150.1-A. The space conditioning system shall be a heat pump, or shall meet the performance compliance requirements of Section 150.1(b)1.

construction_requirements.requirement: Under the 2025 California Residential Code (adopted by Del Mar per DMMC 23.12.060), exterior footings must extend at least 12 inches below undisturbed ground surface (R403.1.4); minimum concrete footing size for one-story light-frame construction per Table R403.1(1) is 12 inches wide by 6 inches thick at 1,500 psf presumptive soil bearing.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Residential Code, R403.1.4 and Table R403.1(1)
citation.title: California Residential Code 2025, Chapter 4 Foundations (UpCodes viewer — non-official mirror, verify against official CRC text)
citation.snippet: Exterior footings shall be placed not less than 12 inches (305 mm) below the undisturbed ground surface.

construction_requirements.requirement: Regional (San Diego County) minimum construction specifications under the 2025 CRC applicable to light-frame accessory-structure foundations: continuous footings and stem walls require minimum two longitudinal No. 4 bars (one top, one bottom) per CRC R403.1.3.3 (a Seismic Design Category D0-D2 provision); slabs-on-grade minimum 3.5 inches thick (CRC R506.1); 10-mil vapor retarder under slab (CRC R506.2.3); 1/2-inch anchor bolts embedded 7 inches at max 6 ft on center with 3x3x0.229-inch plate washers (CRC R403.1.6, R602.11.1).

citation.code_section: CRC R403.1.3.3, R506.1, R506.2.3, R403.1.6 (2025), as applied by County of San Diego PDS #081 (snapshot archived via Wayback capture 2025-04-05)
citation.title: COSD Minimum Construction Specifications (County of San Diego PDS 081, 2025 codes)
citation.snippet: Continuous footings and stem walls shall be provided with a minimum two longitudinal No. 4 bars, one at the top and one at the bottom of the footing. (CRC R403.1.3.3)

construction_requirements.requirement: In Del Mar's Coastal Bluff Overlay Zone, all new principal or accessory structures (including new foundations) must be set back a minimum of 40 feet from the top edge of the coastal bluff, no grading is allowed within 40 feet of the bluff top edge, and any new construction on bluff properties requires a geotechnical report addressing site suitability and bluff stability over a 70-year project life; a Conditional Use Permit and Coastal Development Permit are required before construction.

citation.title: Chapter 30.55 - Coastal Bluff Overlay Zone
citation.snippet: all new or redeveloped principal or accessory structures, including new supporting foundations or supports for existing structures, shall be set back a minimum of 40 feet from the top edge of the coastal bluff

construction_requirements.requirement: For conventional light-frame design under the 2025 California Residential Code in the San Diego region, plans must specify an ultimate design wind speed of 110 mph (CRC R301.2.1) and wind Exposure Category C (CRC R301.2.1.4); Exposure B is acceptable only with site-specific justification. Values come from County of San Diego plan-check practice — Del Mar adopts the same unamended CRC structural provisions; confirm with Del Mar Building Services.

citation.code_section: CRC R301.2.1 and R301.2.1.4 (2025), as applied by County of San Diego PDS #498 (snapshot archived via Wayback capture 2025-08-14)
citation.title: Residential Building Code Plan Check (County of San Diego PDS 498)
citation.snippet: Specify ultimate wind speed = 110 mph per CRC R301.2.1 ○ Specify wind exposure category = C per CRC R301.2.1.4

construction_requirements.requirement: Seismic design category for Del Mar residential construction must be determined site-specifically from mapped spectral response accelerations per CRC Table R301.2.2.1.1 (or CBC 1613 when engineered); coastal San Diego region residential projects are routinely treated as SDC D — the County's baseline specs apply the CRC SDC D0-D2 footing reinforcement provision to all residential foundations — but no official page publishes Del Mar's exact SDC letter; confirm with Del Mar Building Services.

citation.code_section: <<verify>> — CRC Table R301.2.2.1.1 (2025); no official page publishes Del Mar's SDC letter
citation.title: Residential Building Code Plan Check (County of San Diego PDS 498)
citation.snippet: Specify seismic design category per CRC Table R301.2.2.1.1

construction_requirements.requirement: Del Mar local amendment (Ord. 1022, 2025 codes): every new structure in any occupancy group must have a fire-retardant roof covering of at least Class A, and wood shake or shingle roof products are prohibited citywide — this applies to backyard office pods.

citation.code_section: Del Mar Municipal Code 23.12.063 and 23.12.064 (amending CRC R902.1 and R902.1.2)
citation.title: Chapter 23.12 - California Codes for Construction, Local Amendments to CRC R902.1
citation.snippet: No wood shake or shingle products are permitted to be installed on the roof of any Occupancy Group.

construction_requirements.requirement: A Design Review Permit is required for construction of the exterior of all structures in Del Mar; only accessory structures of 48 sq ft or less and 6 ft or less in height qualify for streamlined Administrative Design Review, so a 96-300 sq ft office pod is subject to Design Review Board standards, including requirements to coordinate exterior colors, materials, and architectural form on all elevations and to limit the number of exterior materials (DMMC 23.08.078).

citation.title: Chapter 23.08 - Design Review
citation.snippet: the construction or installation of an accessory structure which measures not more than 48 square feet in area and not more than six feet in height and which meets all required setbacks

construction_requirements.requirement: Del Mar adopted the 2025 California Wildland-Urban Interface Code (Title 24, Part 7) via Ordinance 1022, applying it to the construction of every building or structure in the city per the code's scoping; Del Mar's amendments also state small accessory structures remain regulated by Part 7 even when exempt from a building permit, so WUI ignition-resistant construction provisions can apply to pod materials in designated areas.

citation.code_section: Del Mar Municipal Code 23.12.100 (and 23.12.062)
citation.title: Chapter 23.12 - California Codes for Construction, Division 10 - Wildland-Urban Interface Code
citation.snippet: the 2025 California Wildland-Urban Interface Code, Part 7, Title 24 of the California Code of Regulations, a portion of the California Building Standards Code

authority.dept: City of Del Mar Planning & Community Development — Building Services Division

jurisdictions.adopted_codes: Per DMMC Ch. 23.12 (Ord. 1022, eff. Oct 22, 2025): 2025 California Building, Electrical, Plumbing, Mechanical, Energy, Residential, and Green codes.

citation.title: Del Mar Municipal Code Ch. 23.05 Construction Permits (defers to state code exemptions)
citation.snippet: meets an exemption per the California Building Code or Title 23

rules.rationale: DMMC defers to the state CRC R105.2 exemption (one-story detached accessory structures up to 120 sqft), but that exemption is written for 'tool and storage sheds, playhouses and similar uses' — a conditioned, occupied office may not qualify, so confirm with Building. Even if the shell is exempt: the 50A circuit needs an Electrical Permit (DMMC 23.05.040.A.1), Design Review applies to exterior structures (Ch. 23.08), and a coastal exemption determination is needed (Ch. 30.75). Shipped as 'depends' per the safe-error guardrail.

citation.title: Del Mar Municipal Code Ch. 23.05 Construction Permits
citation.snippet: No structure shall be erected, constructed, enlarged, altered

rules.rationale: The Sixteen and Station exceed the 120 sqft state exemption, so a Building Permit is required, plus Electrical Permit for the circuit and a Mechanical Permit for heating/cooling equipment (23.05.040.A.3). Design Review (Ch. 23.08) and coastal review (Ch. 30.75) apply on top.

citation.title: Del Mar Municipal Code Ch. 23.05 Construction Permits (Plumbing Permits)
citation.snippet: unless a Plumbing Permit has been obtained

rules.rationale: A Plumbing Permit is required for any plumbing system installed within or on any structure or premises. Plumbing also defeats the state shed exemption, so a Building Permit applies at any footprint. A pod with kitchenette/bath plus sleeping risks ADU classification (DMMC Ch. 30.91), which triggers an Administrative Coastal Development Permit.

citation.title: Del Mar Municipal Code Ch. 30.91 (June 2025) — ADU Regulations
citation.snippet: requires issuance of an Administrative Coastal Development permit

rules.rationale: A detached unit used for sleeping points toward ADU treatment: all construction permits per Ch. 23.04/23.05, and ADU approval requires an Administrative Coastal Development Permit — new detached units do not meet the conversion/attached CDP exemptions. Del Mar permits 'tiny homes' on any lot that allows an ADU. Confirm classification with Planning.

citation.title: Del Mar Municipal Code — Supplemental Regulations / Manufactured Homes / CDP definitions
citation.snippet: boats, trailers, trucks, campers or the like be stored or parked

rules.rationale: No DMMC provision squarely addresses occupied trailer-mounted office units. Trailers/campers are barred from required front/side yards; manufactured homes need permanent foundations; and the CDP 'development' definition is broad enough to cover a parked, occupied pod. Get a written determination from Planning before relying on wheels to avoid permits.

jurisdictions.zoning_note: COASTAL: Del Mar implements a certified Local Coastal Program citywide — DMMC 30.75.040.A requires a Coastal Development Permit for all 'development' (broadly defined). Key exemption: improvements to existing single-family residences incl. 'storage sheds' (30.75.200.C) — a backyard pod plausibly fits, BUT the exemption is lost on beaches/wetlands, within 50 ft of coastal bluffs, in the Lagoon Overlay, near steep slopes, or in the CDP Appeals Area. Every pod placement needs at minimum a city coastal exemption determination; lot location decides whether a full CDP is required. ADUs always need an Administrative CDP. SETBACKS (example, R1-10 zone): front 20 ft, rear 25 ft, interior side 7.5 ft; max height 26 ft (14 ft in Bluff/Slope/Canyon Overlay). The 500-sqft accessory-building rear-yard encroachment allowance (30.86.200(C)) EXCLUDES the R1-40/R1-14/R1-10 zones. DESIGN REVIEW: DMMC 23.08.030 requires a Design Review Permit for exterior construction of all structures; the administrative shortcut caps at 48 sqft and 6 ft height — every Mini·O pod exceeds it.
jurisdictions.hoa_note: An HOA may impose separate requirements regardless of city rules.
jurisdictions.notes: DRAFT (researched 2026-06-09, DMMC read via Municode's public content API — human-readable equivalents at library.municode.com/ca/del_mar, Supp. 11 Update 1, codified through Ord. 1027, Feb 17, 2026). Del Mar layers FOUR gates on a backyard pod: building/trade permits, Design Review, coastal exemption-or-CDP, and (if sleeping) ADU + Administrative CDP. Separate Electrical, Plumbing, and Mechanical permits are required for any new wiring, plumbing, or HVAC even on an exempt shell. The 'entirely within the Coastal Zone' claim could not be verified on a fetched page <<verify>> — but the citywide CDP chapter makes coastal review the operative assumption. Original research posture for the under-120 rule was building_permit: likely_exempt (medium); shipped as 'depends' per the safe-error guardrail. Validation anchor: live permit (Htut Zaw).

research.method: DMMC chapters fetched via api.municode.com (library.municode.com blocks automation); city pages from delmar.ca.us; CRC R105.2 text verified via a fetched ca.gov county handout quoting it. See research/REVIEW.md.
caveats: Citation URLs are Municode content-API URLs (the pages actually fetched); human-readable pages at library.municode.com use the same nodeIds.
Del Mar's 2019 construction-permit guidelines PDF is 404 and not cited.
Coastal Zone full-coverage claim unverified on a fetched page.
Original research posture for under-120 was likely_exempt (medium); shipped as depends per safe-error guardrail.

jurisdictions.name: Unincorporated King County

construction_requirements.requirement: Conditioned buildings in King County (Climate Zone Marine 4) must meet 2021 WSEC-R Table R402.1.3 prescriptive minimums: ceiling R-60, wood-frame wall R-20 cavity + R-5 continuous (or R-13 + R-10 ci), floor R-30, slab-on-grade R-10 to 4 ft depth, fenestration U-0.30 max, skylight U-0.50 max.

citation.code_section: 2021 WSEC-R Table R402.1.3
citation.title: Washington State Energy Code - Residential, 2021 Edition (Chapter 51-11R WAC), 2nd Ed. Jan 2024
citation.snippet: INSULATION MINIMUM R-VALUES AND FENESTRATION REQUIREMENTS BY COMPONENTS ... CLIMATE ZONE 5 AND MARINE 4 ... Ceiling R-Value 60 ... Wood Frame Wall R-Value 20+5 or 13+10 ... Floor R-Value 30 ... Slab R-Value & Depth 10, 4 ft

construction_requirements.requirement: As an alternative to R-values, assemblies may comply with 2021 WSEC-R Table R402.1.2 maximum U-factors (Climate Zone 5 and Marine 4): fenestration U-0.30, skylight U-0.50, ceiling U-0.024, above-grade wall U-0.056, floor U-0.029, slab-on-grade F-0.54. Total-UA trade-off compliance is also permitted per R402.1.5.

citation.code_section: 2021 WSEC-R Table R402.1.2
citation.title: Washington State Energy Code - Residential, 2021 Edition (Chapter 51-11R WAC), 2nd Ed. Jan 2024
citation.snippet: CLIMATE ZONE 5 AND MARINE 4 Fenestration U-Factor 0.30 Skylight U-Factor 0.50 Ceiling U-Factor 0.024 Above-Grade Wall U-Factor 0.056 Floor U-Factor 0.029 Slab on Grade F-Factor 0.54

construction_requirements.requirement: A heated/cooled (conditioned) backyard office pod is inside the WSEC building thermal envelope provisions: the 2021 WSEC-R exempts only buildings that do not contain conditioned space, have peak space-conditioning energy use under 3.4 Btu/h per sq ft (1.0 W/sq ft), or are isolated greenhouses. A pod with a heat source exceeding that threshold must meet full envelope requirements.

citation.code_section: 2021 WSEC-R R402.1 Exception
citation.title: Washington State Energy Code - Residential, 2021 Edition (Chapter 51-11R WAC), 2nd Ed. Jan 2024
citation.snippet: shall be exempt from the building thermal envelope provisions of this code: 1. Those with a peak design rate of energy usage less than 3.4 Btu/h • ft 2 (10.7 W/m2) or 1.0 watt/ft2 of floor area for space conditioning purposes. 2. Those that do not contain conditioned space.

construction_requirements.requirement: The 2021 Washington State Energy Code applies to King County permit applications deemed complete on or after March 15, 2024 (statewide effective date). King County's own 2025 energy code amendments apply only to commercial and 4+ story multifamily buildings, so a residential accessory pod follows the unamended state 2021 WSEC-R.

citation.code_section: 2021 WSEC (WAC 51-11R) adoption
citation.title: King County energy code - King County, Washington
citation.snippet: The 2021 Washington State Energy Code (WSEC) is effective for applications deemed complete on or after March 15, 2024.

construction_requirements.requirement: King County Permitting requires residential construction plans to include a WSEC energy compliance sheet covering heat-sizing calculations, WSEC R406 energy improvement credits, and IRC indoor air quality (ventilation) requirements (Residential Basics Program submittal requirements, April 2026 handout).

citation.code_section: King County Residential Basics Program submittal requirements
citation.title: Residential Basics Program (King County Permitting handout, April 2026)
citation.snippet: An energy code specification sheet that addresses Washington State Energy Code compliance requirements including heat size calculation, energy code improvement credits and International Residential Code indoor air quality requirements.

construction_requirements.requirement: Exterior footings must extend at least 12 inches below undisturbed ground surface, and footings for light-frame construction must be not less than 12 inches wide (sized per IRC Table R403.1(1) by soil bearing and snow load), per the Washington State Residential Code (2021 IRC as amended). King County publishes no deeper local frost-depth amendment; the 12-inch IRC minimum governs in the lowlands.

citation.code_section: WA Residential Code (2021) R403.1.4 / R403.1.1
citation.title: Chapter 4 Foundations: Washington State Residential Code 2021 | UpCodes (non-official mirror — verify against WAC 51-51)
citation.snippet: Exterior footings shall be placed not less than 12 inches (305 mm) below the undisturbed ground surface.

construction_requirements.requirement: Frost protection is not required for freestanding light-frame accessory structures of 600 sq ft or less with eave height 10 ft or less (WA Residential Code 2021, R403.1.4.1 Exception 1) — which covers typical 96-300 sq ft office pods; the 12-inch minimum footing embedment of R403.1.4 still applies.

citation.code_section: WA Residential Code (2021) R403.1.4.1 Exception 1
citation.title: Chapter 4 Foundations: Washington State Residential Code 2021 | UpCodes (non-official mirror — verify against WAC 51-51)
citation.snippet: Protection of free-standing accessory structures with an area of 600 square feet (56 m2) or less, of light-frame construction, with an eave height of 10 feet (3048 mm) or less shall not be required.

construction_requirements.requirement: The minimum roof design snow load in unincorporated King County is 25 psf for all buildings and structures (KCC 16.04.410; reaffirmed in Public Rule 16-04-500/506 and in county plan-submittal design criteria).

citation.title: King County Code Title 16 - Building and Construction Standards
citation.snippet: The minimum roof design snow load shall be 25 pounds per square feet.

construction_requirements.requirement: Ground snow load is elevation-dependent: Pg = Cg x h(msl), where Cg is the isoline coefficient from the King County Ground Snow Load Map (e.g., Seattle/Renton/Enumclaw 0.05, Issaquah 0.054, Duvall 0.056, Fall City 0.073, North Bend 0.075, Skykomish 0.094) and h(msl) is site elevation in feet. Sites over 1,000 ft elevation must also consider drift and sliding snow; roof snow load may never be below 25 psf.

citation.code_section: King County Public Rule 16-04-506
citation.title: 16-04-500 -- 16-04-506 King County Public Rules: Structural Loading - Minimum Roof Snow Loads
citation.snippet: The ground snow load, Pg, shall be determined in accordance with the following formula: Pg = Cghmsl ... For all buildings and structures in King County, the roof snow load shall not be less than 25 psf.

construction_requirements.requirement: King County Permitting's published residential structural design criteria specify a design wind speed of 110 mph with Exposure C for lateral and gravity design of one- and two-family structures in unincorporated King County (Residential Basics Program handout, April 2026, under 2021 codes).

citation.code_section: King County Residential Basics Program - structural design criteria
citation.title: Residential Basics Program (King County Permitting handout, April 2026)
citation.snippet: Structural calculations for lateral and gravity design, including design criteria: Seismic design category of D2, Wind speed of 110 mph, Exposure C, Roof snow load 25 lbs. per square foot minimum.

construction_requirements.requirement: Residential structures in unincorporated King County are designed to Seismic Design Category D2, per King County Permitting's published structural design criteria (Residential Basics Program handout, April 2026).

citation.code_section: King County Residential Basics Program - structural design criteria
citation.title: Residential Basics Program (King County Permitting handout, April 2026)
citation.snippet: Seismic design category of D2

construction_requirements.requirement: Drainage review under the King County Surface Water Design Manual is triggered when a project results in 2,000 sq ft or more of new-plus-replaced impervious surface, or 7,000 sq ft or more of land-disturbing activity (KCC 9.04.030). A 96-300 sq ft pod alone is usually below the threshold, but the count includes all new plus replaced impervious surface on the project site, so site totals must be checked.

citation.title: King County Code Title 9 - Surface Water Management
citation.snippet: Would result in two thousand square feet or more of new impervious surface, replaced impervious surface or new plus replaced impervious surface

construction_requirements.requirement: The 2021 code editions (IBC, IRC, WSEC and related state codes) govern: the state-adopted 2021 codes became effective March 15, 2024, and King County's March 2025 ordinance updates KCC Title 16 references from the 2018 to the 2021 editions. Note: the codified KCC 16.02.110 text online still recites the 2018 edition — the state 2021 adoption controls regardless.

citation.code_section: KCC 16.02.110 (2025 update ordinance)
citation.title: Summary of Proposed Ordinance relating to King County Building and Fire Code Updates (March 24, 2025)
citation.snippet: The proposed ordinance reflects the most recent 2021 international codes, as adopted by the state, which became effective March 15, 2024.

authority.dept: King County Dept. of Local Services, Permitting Division (plumbing via Public Health — Seattle & King County; electrical via WA State L&I)

jurisdictions.adopted_codes: Per KCC 16.02.110: 2018 IBC/IRC effective Feb 1, 2021, plus IECC/IMC/UPC. A March 2025 county ordinance summary updates references to the 2021 editions (WA state codes effective March 15, 2024) — enacted status <<verify>>.

citation.code_section: KCC 16.02.240 (amending the IRC/IBC work-exempt-from-permit list)
citation.title: Residential Accessory Building or Miscellaneous Structures Permit (DLS Permitting, July 2020)
citation.snippet: less than 200 square feet and unconditioned space

rules.rationale: King County's shed exemption is two-pronged: under 200 sqft AND unconditioned. The county's 2025 code-update summary is explicit: 'Detached accessory structures must be unconditioned to be exempt.' Mini·O pods are insulated and heated/cooled, so even the Twelve and Sixteen likely need a building permit. Electrical permit for the 50A circuit comes from WA L&I regardless. Exempt structures must still meet KCC 21A.12 setbacks.

citation.title: King County Code Title 16 / Do you need a permit?
citation.snippet: A storage shed with a floor area of 200 square feet or less

rules.rationale: The exemption caps at 200 sqft, so the Station (300 sqft) always needs a Residential Accessory Building permit (apply via MyBuildingPermit.com). Factory-built structures also need WA L&I Factory Built Structures approval — and a county building permit is still required to site the unit.

citation.code_section: KCC Title 16 (UPC as adopted, KCC 16.32) — permits issued by Public Health — Seattle & King County
citation.title: Plumbing and gas piping applications and permits — Public Health, Seattle & King County
citation.snippet: Seattle and King County codes require that you obtain a permit

rules.rationale: Public Health — Seattle & King County issues plumbing permits in unincorporated King County (206-477-8050 Eastgate office). Adding a bath/kitchenette also triggers water supply / sewage review — on septic, expect On-Site Sewage System review and possibly a sewer certificate.

citation.title: Residential Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs) — DLS Permits (March 2026)
citation.snippet: shall not exceed one thousand square feet of heated floor area

rules.rationale: A sleeping pod with sanitation/kitchen functions as an ADU: full building permit as a dwelling, notice on title recorded before approval, max 1,000 sqft heated, one ADU per rural lot (two in the urban growth area), prohibited in Forest zones, public health/sewer or septic review. Any residence regardless of size must be anchored to a permanent foundation and meet energy code.

citation.code_section: King County zoning code RV definition (per DLS tiny-homes bulletin)
citation.title: Residential Tiny Homes (DLS Permitting Division, March 2020)
citation.snippet: Tiny homes on wheels are treated like recreational vehicles

rules.rationale: Wheeled units are treated like RVs: not dwelling units, no full-time residence, camping capped at 60 days per rolling 365. A trailer-mounted OFFICE pod is not directly addressed — the county could treat a permanently sited, utility-connected wheeled pod as a structure. The premises wiring for the 50A circuit still needs an L&I electrical permit. Confirm with the Permitting Division.

jurisdictions.zoning_note: All structures, including permit-exempt ones, must meet KCC Title 21A dimensional standards (setbacks in KCC 21A.12; exact numeric setbacks per zone in the 21A.12.030 tables <<verify>> — table text not directly archived). Critical areas (wetlands, steep slopes, streams) void the shed exemption entirely. ADUs: KCC 21A.08.030 B.7.a — 1,000 sqft heated cap, notice on title, one per rural lot / two in urban area, prohibited in Forest zones.
jurisdictions.hoa_note: An HOA may impose separate requirements regardless of city rules.
jurisdictions.notes: DRAFT (researched 2026-06-09, official kingcounty.gov / lni.wa.gov / sbcc.wa.gov pages). Three different authorities: building permits — King County DLS Permitting (206-296-6600, MyBuildingPermit.com); electrical — WA State Labor & Industries; plumbing — Public Health Seattle & King County. THE HEADLINE: the 200 sqft exemption requires UNCONDITIONED space, so every conditioned Mini·O pod should be assumed to need a county building permit at any size, an L&I electrical permit, and a PHSKC plumbing permit if wet. Factory-built units need L&I Factory Built Structures approval plus the county siting permit. Septic (OSS) capacity and critical-areas review are common complications in rural zones.

research.method: Official county/state pages and DLS bulletins fetched and read; see research/REVIEW.md.
caveats: KCC Title 21A setback tables too large to extract — exact numeric setbacks not verified.
2021-code-edition update ordinance: only the March 2025 plain-language summary fetched; enacted status unverified.
Full KCC 16.02.240 exemption text summarized via Title 16 page and ordinance summary, not independently read.

jurisdictions.name: Unincorporated Los Angeles County

construction_requirements.requirement: Under the 2025 California Energy Code (Title 24 Part 6, effective for LA County's 2026 code cycle), mandatory wall insulation for wood-framed walls is maximum U-0.095 for 2x4 framing (minimum R-15 at 16 in. o.c.) and maximum U-0.069 for 2x6 framing (minimum R-21 at 16 in. o.c.) per Section 150.0(c). Applies to a conditioned detached accessory structure such as an office pod.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code § 150.0(c)
citation.title: CEC 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards — What's New for Single-Family Residential
citation.snippet: Updates mandatory wall insulation maximum 0.095 U-factor for 2 x 4 inch wood framing (minimum R-15 with 16" on center) and maximum 0.069 U-factor for 2 x 6 wood-framing (minimum R-21 with 16" on center). § 150.0(c)

construction_requirements.requirement: 2025 Energy Code prescriptive ceiling insulation: ventilated attics require minimum R-38 in climate zones 1 and 8-16 (which includes climate zone 9 covering most of the unincorporated LA basin) and R-30 in zones 2-7; cathedral ceilings require minimum R-38 in all climate zones. Table 150.1-A Option C.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code § 150.1(c)1Aiii / Table 150.1-A
citation.title: CEC 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards — What's New for Single-Family Residential
citation.snippet: Updates prescriptive Option C of Table 150.1-A ventilated attic minimum R-38 in climate zones 1, 8-16, minimum R-30 in climates zones 2-7. Adds cathedral ceilings minimum R-38 with no radiant barrier in all climate zones

construction_requirements.requirement: 2025 Energy Code fenestration: mandatory maximum weighted-average U-factor 0.40 including skylights (Section 150.0(q)); prescriptive maximum U-factor 0.30 in climate zones 6-10 and 15 (covers LA basin CZ 9) and 0.27 in zones 1-5, 11-14, 16.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code § 150.0(q), § 150.1(c)3A
citation.title: CEC 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards — What's New for Single-Family Residential
citation.snippet: Updates prescriptive maximum U-factor 0.27 for fenestration in Climate Zones 1-5, 11-14, 16; maximum U-factor 0.30 in Climate Zones 6-10, 15

construction_requirements.requirement: 2025 Energy Code prescriptive path requires heating equipment to be a heat pump in all climate zones (or meet the performance path), with refrigerant charge verification for heat pumps in all climate zones — relevant to mini-split-conditioned office pods.

citation.code_section: 2025 California Energy Code § 150.1(c)6-7
citation.title: CEC 2025 Building Energy Efficiency Standards — What's New for Single-Family Residential
citation.snippet: Updates prescriptive requirements: heating equipment to be heat pump in all Climate Zones or meet performance requirements in § 150.1(b)1; refrigerant charge verification for heat pumps in all Climate Zones

construction_requirements.requirement: For a one-story light-frame building, LA County's 2026 Residential Code plan review requires continuous footings 12 inches wide, 6 inches thick, and minimum 12 inches deep below natural ground surface or certified fill grade (15 in. wide for two stories), per Table R403.1(1) and Table 1809.7. Allowable soil bearing is limited to 1,500 psf unless a soils report justifies more (Table R401.4.1(1)).

citation.code_section: 2026 LA County Residential Code T-R403.1(1), T-1809.7; T-R401.4.1(1)
citation.title: County of Los Angeles Residential Plan Review 2026 (One- & Two-Family Dwelling Plan Review List)
citation.snippet: For foundation supporting (1) / (2) floors, sections (12) / (15) inches wide, 6 inches thick and minimum 12 inches in depth below natural ground surface or certified fill grade are required. (T-R403.1 (1), T-1809.7)

construction_requirements.requirement: LA County presumes expansive soil by default: unless an approved soils report shows soil is not expansive by the Expansion Index Test, foundations must have continuous exterior footings extending 24 in. below grade (18 in. at interior bearing walls), four continuous #4 bars, a 4-in. slab with #4 bars at 16 in. o.c. each way over a 10-mil moisture barrier sandwiched in sand fill, and 18-in.-deep pre-saturation of soil before the pour (Residential Code Manual R401.4 Art. 1).

citation.code_section: LA County RCM 401.4 Art. 1 (2026 Residential Plan Review item 111)
citation.title: County of Los Angeles Residential Plan Review 2026 (One- & Two-Family Dwelling Plan Review List)
citation.snippet: Foundation and floor slabs shall conform to the following requirements, unless an approved soils report indicates that soil is not expansive by the Expansion Index Test method, or recommends other details: (RCM 401.4 Art. 1) a. Continuous footings under exterior walls and interior bearing walls extending below grade 24-in. and 18-in. respectively

construction_requirements.requirement: Grading permit triggers (2026 LACBC Appendix J103): any excavation exceeding 2 ft deep (except footings, basements, retaining walls); fills intended to support structures; fills obstructing drainage; fills 1 ft+ on natural slopes steeper than 5:1; fills 3 ft+ deep and over 50 cu yd; fills 5 ft+ deep and over 20 cu yd. Buildings on or into slopes steeper than 1:3 (33.3%) must comply with the county hillside provisions and get supplemental Hillside Structures plan review.

citation.code_section: 2026 LACBC Appendix J103; R301.1.3 (plan review items 9, 86, 12c)
citation.title: County of Los Angeles Residential Plan Review 2026 (One- & Two-Family Dwelling Plan Review List)
citation.snippet: A grading permit is required for the following: a. All excavations exceeding 2-ft. in depth (except for footings, basements and retaining walls)

construction_requirements.requirement: LA County BCM #95 ground snow load criteria by elevation: under 2,000 ft — no snow load; 2,000-3,500 ft — 20 psf; 3,500-5,000 ft — 40 psf; over 5,000 ft — 80 psf. Applies to pods in mountain areas. Note: the BCM is based on the 2011 LACBC/ASCE 7-05 and has not been reissued for the 2026 code; verify with plan check.

citation.code_section: LA County BCM #95, Section 1608 Article 1
citation.title: Building Code Manual #95 — Snow Loads (County of Los Angeles DPW Building and Safety)
citation.snippet: Between 2,000 and 3,500 feet 20 lbs./sq. ft Snow loads may be excluded in the calculation of the seismic forces.

construction_requirements.requirement: LA County BCM #11 policy: buildings shall be designed for a minimum 85 mph (3-second gust) basic wind speed throughout Los Angeles County, including the mapped 'special wind region' in the southern county. Note: this BCM is based on the 2011 LACBC/ASCE 7-05; the 2026 code performs wind analysis per ASCE 7-22 (LACBC 1609), whose ultimate design wind speeds are numerically higher — the current ultimate-speed figure was not verifiable on an official page.

citation.code_section: LA County BCM #11, Section 1609.1.1 Article 1; 2026 LACBC 1609 / ASCE 7-22
citation.title: Building Code Manual #11 — ASCE 7-05 Special Wind Region Loads (County of Los Angeles DPW)
citation.snippet: Buildings, structures, and parts thereof shall be designed to withstand a minimum wind load based on an 85 mph (3-second gust) basic wind speed throughout Los Angeles County, including the "special wind region"

construction_requirements.requirement: LA County one- and two-family construction is reviewed under Seismic Design Categories D0, D1, or D2 (SDC E requires full engineering unless reclassified per R301.2.2.1.2). Practical effects for a pod: seismic bracing provisions of CRC R301.2.2 apply; foundation anchor bolts must be 1/2-in. diameter (5/8-in. for SDC E/F), embedded 7 in., spaced max 6 ft, with 0.229 x 3 in. plate washers; lateral design uses the more restrictive of wind or seismic per LACBC 1609/1613 and ASCE 7-22.

citation.code_section: 2026 LA County Residential Code R301.2.2, R301.2.2.1, R403.1.6; LACBC 1609, 1613
citation.title: County of Los Angeles Residential Plan Review 2026 (One- & Two-Family Dwelling Plan Review List)
citation.snippet: All one- and two-family dwellings and townhouses in Seismic Design Category D0, D1 or D2 shall comply with seismic provisions in Section R301.2.2.

construction_requirements.requirement: For 2026, LA County wildfire construction standards moved from CBC Chapter 7A to the 2025 California Wildland-Urban Interface Code (Part 7), Chapter 5, applied via the 2026 LA County Building Code to all new buildings in WUI Fire Areas / Fire Hazard Severity Zones. Key pod-relevant requirements: Class A roof covering; ember-resistant vents; ignition-resistant/noncombustible or 1-hr-rated exterior walls; multi-pane exterior glazing with at least one tempered pane. Exception: Group U accessory structures not exceeding 120 sq ft (an occupied office pod over 120 sq ft does not qualify).

citation.code_section: 2025 CA Wildland-Urban Interface Code Ch. 5 (§§ 501.1, 504.2, 504.8, 504.10) via 2026 LACBC
citation.title: LA County Building & Safety Standard Construction Notes — Fire Hazard Severity Zone (2026)
citation.snippet: Roof covering shall be Class A fire classification when tested in accordance with ASTM E108 or UL790.

construction_requirements.requirement: Construction-geometry limits on the permit exemption: a one-story detached accessory building is exempt from a building permit only if gross floor area does not exceed 120 sq ft, height does not exceed 12 ft, and maximum roof projection does not exceed 24 in. Electrical, plumbing, or mechanical equipment still requires separate permits, and zoning applies regardless of size — so a conditioned 50A office pod always needs at least electrical/mechanical permits even under 120 sq ft.

citation.code_section: 2026 LACBC / Title 26 permit exemptions (LA County Public Works FAQ)
citation.title: LA County Public Works FAQ — I've been told that I don't need permits for buildings that are 120 sq. ft. or less. Is that true?
citation.snippet: the gross floor area does not exceed 120 square feet, the height does not exceed 12 feet, and the maximum roof projection does not exceed 24 inches

construction_requirements.requirement: Placement constraint for pods near lot lines (2026 LA County Residential Code R302.1): exterior walls of accessory buildings closer than 5 ft (non-sprinklered) or 3 ft (sprinklered) to the property line must be 1-hour fire-resistance-rated; no openings permitted in walls less than 3 ft from the property line; openings limited to 25% of wall area between 3 and 5 ft (non-sprinklered); eave projections prohibited within 2 ft of the property line.

citation.code_section: 2026 LA County Residential Code R302.1, T-R302.1(1) & (2)
citation.title: County of Los Angeles Residential Plan Review 2026 (One- & Two-Family Dwelling Plan Review List)
citation.snippet: Exterior walls of dwellings and accessory buildings less than 5-ft.(non-Sprinklered) / 3-ft. (Sprinklered) to the property line shall be 1-hour fire-resistance-rated construction. (R302.1, T-R302.1(1) & (2))

authority.dept: LA County Public Works, Building and Safety Division (permits via EPIC-LA and district offices)

jurisdictions.adopted_codes: 2026 County of LA Building Code (Title 26), effective Jan 1, 2026, adopting the 2025 California Building Code with county amendments; companion Titles 27 (Electrical), 28 (Plumbing), 29 (Mechanical), 30 (Residential). Note: the permit-exemption provision is numbered Title 26 Sec. 106.3, not model-code 105.2.

citation.code_section: LA County Building Code (Title 26) Sec. 106.3, item 1
citation.title: Work Exempt from Building Permits — LA County Building and Safety
citation.snippet: gross floor area does not exceed 120 square feet

rules.rationale: One-story detached accessory buildings are exempt only if gross floor area is 120 sqft or less, height 12 ft or less, and roof projection 24 in or less — and the exemption is written for tool/storage sheds, playhouses, and 'similar uses.' A conditioned office may be questioned, so confirm use with the district office; shipped as 'depends' per the safe-error guardrail. The exemption does NOT waive the electrical permit for the 50A circuit or zoning compliance (Title 22 applies to all structures).

citation.code_section: LA County Building Code (Title 26) Sec. 106.3 (exemption inapplicable over 120 sqft)
citation.title: Work Exempt from Building Permits — LA County Building and Safety
citation.snippet: does not exceed 120 square feet, the height does not exceed 12 feet

rules.rationale: Over 120 sqft the exemption cannot apply — building permit from Building and Safety required (the Sixteen and Station). Regional Planning approval is needed before the building permit issues for accessory structures over 120 sqft. Applications on/after Jan 1, 2026 are reviewed under the 2025-cycle county codes.

citation.code_section: LA County Public Works construction permits page; Title 26 Sec. 106.3 exemption limited to shed-type uses
citation.title: Construction Permits — LA County Building and Safety
citation.snippet: New, expanded, or replaced plumbing items

rules.rationale: A plumbing permit is required for new plumbing regardless of structure size — separate trade permits apply even in exempt sheds. A bathroom/kitchenette also moves the structure outside the shed-type exemption toward habitable-space review. With sleeping + kitchen + bath, the unit meets the county ADU definition and must be permitted as an ADU.

citation.code_section: LA County Code Title 22, Sec. 22.140.640 (ADU development standards)
citation.title: Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) Summary — LA County Planning
citation.snippet: a sleeping area, kitchen, and bathroom

rules.rationale: Sleeping use makes the pod habitable space — never within the shed exemption. With sleeping area + kitchen + bathroom it is an ADU under the county ordinance (ministerial approval per state ADU law). Sleeping without kitchen/bath still requires habitable-space review — confirm classification with Building and Safety and Regional Planning ((213) 974-6411).

citation.code_section: LA County Planning zoning enforcement guidance (Title 22)
citation.title: Common Zoning Violations — LA County Planning
citation.snippet: Living in a recreational vehicle or trailer for any length of time

rules.rationale: No county page exempts trailer-mounted units as vehicles, and unincorporated LA County has no published movable-tiny-home provision (unlike the City of LA). Enforcement flags occupied trailers as violations — an occupied trailer-mounted pod risks being treated as illegal trailer occupancy rather than escaping permits. Confirm with Regional Planning and Building and Safety.

jurisdictions.zoning_note: Title 22 (Planning and Zoning) applies regardless of building-permit exemption. County enforcement examples show 5-ft side / 15-ft rear residential setbacks (exact values vary by zone and community standards district). ADU standards: Title 22 Sec. 22.140.640 (the 4-ft side/rear ADU setback appears only in search snippets, not a fetched page <<verify>>). Regional Planning approval precedes the building permit for accessory structures over 120 sqft. Regional Planning: (213) 974-6411, info@planning.lacounty.gov.
jurisdictions.hoa_note: An HOA may impose separate requirements regardless of city rules.
jurisdictions.notes: DRAFT (researched 2026-06-09, official pw.lacounty.gov / planning.lacounty.gov / energy.ca.gov pages). The 96 sqft Twelve with no plumbing/sleeping is the only configuration plausibly exempt from a building permit (height limit 12 ft, roof projection limit 24 in) — but the 50A feed always triggers an electrical permit, and the conditioned-office use question led us to ship the building outcome as 'depends' (original research posture: likely_exempt, high). Permits issue via EPIC-LA (epicla.lacounty.gov). Factory-built/state-insignia treatment not researched on a fetched page <<verify>>. The exemption lives at Title 26 Sec. 106.3 (county renumbering of model-code 105.2).

research.method: Official county pages and the Title 26 adopting-ordinance PDF fetched and read; see research/REVIEW.md.
caveats: Codified Title 26 Sec. 106.3 text unfetchable on Municode (403) — corroborated via county workexempt page and adopting-ordinance PDF.
ADU 4-ft setback figure from search snippets only.
Original research posture for under-120 was likely_exempt (high); shipped as depends per safe-error guardrail.

template.hoa_note: An HOA may impose separate requirements regardless of city rules.
